description_long: This object is a vintage metal kettle with a wide, rounded body and a long, cylindrical spout. The body is perforated with numerous small holes arranged in concentric circles, suggesting it may function as a strainer or watering can rather than a traditional kettle for boiling water. The surface appears weathered and matte, with a grayish patina typical of aged metal. There is no visible handle or lid, and the spout is directly attached to the side of the main body. The base is flat, allowing the object to stand upright on a surface. The overall design is utilitarian and plain, with no decorative embellishments.
description: A weathered, gray metal kettle with a perforated, rounded body and a long cylindrical spout, lacking a handle or lid.
description_view_0: View from the open end of the spout, showing the circular mouth and the flat base of the kettle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long cylindrical spout and the rounded, perforated body.
description_view_2: View of the perforated base, displaying the concentric arrangement of holes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the spout and the rounded, perforated body.
